channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Male sex and Hispanic ethnicity linked to higher early-onset colorectal cancer risk
item.description: Male sex and Hispanic ethnicity were associated with increased risk of early-onset colorectal cancer. Among females, higher birthweight also contributed.

item.title: Efforts to end school vaccine mandates stall in Florida
item.description: Every state and Washington, D.C., requires certain vaccinations for school attendance. These mandates have faced resistance in Florida.

item.title: Old theory offers new approach to tackling doctor burnout
item.description: Research suggests self-determination theory can improve provider well-being and patient care, according to Medscape Medical News.

item.title: Q&A: Sunscreen safety and essential skin protection tips
item.description: Dr. Michael Girardi learned the importance of sunscreen early after his uncle died of melanoma.
